items.title: Migrate codebase from Python 2 to Python 3
items.body:
Both `main.py` and `experimental.py` used Python 2-only constructs (`izip`, `print` statements, `raw_input`, unguarded `map()`). This migrates the codebase to Python 3.

### `main.py`
- Drop `izip` import; use built-in `zip`
- `print "..."` → `print(...)`
- `raw_input(...)` → `input(...)`
- `map(int, ...)` → `list(map(int, ...))` (map is lazy in Py3)
- Integer division: `/` → `//`

### `experimental.py`
- `raw_input(...)` → `input(...)`

### `README.md`
- Requirement updated from Python 2 → Python 3
- Commands updated from `python` → `python3`

items.title: Fix CI: Python 3.10 compatibility and black formatting drift
items.body:
Two CI failures on the `copilot/fix-module-not-found-error` branch: `datetime.UTC` (Python 3.11+) caused `ImportError` on the Python 3.10 runners, and 9 files had accumulated Black formatting drift.

## Changes

- **`datetime.UTC` → `timezone.utc`** across all affected files — `datetime.UTC` was introduced in Python 3.11; replaced with `timezone.utc` (available since Python 3.2) to restore 3.10 compatibility:
  ```python
  # Before (Python 3.11+ only)
  from datetime import UTC, datetime
  return datetime.now(UTC)

  # After
  from datetime import datetime, timezone
  return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
  ```
  Files affected: `auth/model.py`, `finance/models.py`, `finance/routes.py`, `finance/service.py`, `tests/test_finance.py`

- **Black formatting** — ran `black .` to bring all 9 drifted files into conformance with the enforced style check

items.title: fix: Intel GPU Dockerfile Python 3.12 compatibility
items.body:
## Problem

The `Dockerfile.gpu-intel` fails to build on the `intel/oneapi-runtime:latest` base image (Ubuntu Noble, Python 3.12) due to three issues:

### 1. Broken apt dependencies
`intel-level-zero-gpu` has unmet dependencies (`libigc1`, `libigdfcl1`) that are not installable. These packages (`intel-opencl-icd`, `intel-level-zero-gpu`, `level-zero`) are already present in the `intel/oneapi-runtime` base image.

### 2. Python 3.12 pkgutil.ImpImporter removal
`numpy==1.24.0` and old `setuptools` use `pkgutil.ImpImporter` which was [removed in Python 3.12](https://docs.python.org/3.12/whatsnew/3.12.html#removed). This causes:
```
AttributeError: module 'pkgutil' has no attribute 'ImpImporter'. Did you mean: 'zipimporter'?
```

### 3. GPU acceleration display
`inference.py` checks `torch.cuda.is_available()` for the GPU acceleration log message, which returns `False` on Intel XPU even though the GPU is being used.

## Changes

- **Dockerfile.gpu-intel**: Remove redundant Intel GPU packages from apt install
- **Dockerfile.gpu-intel**: Add `pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel` before torch
- **requirements.txt**: Bump numpy from `==1.24.0` to `>=1.26.0`
- **tts_engine/inference.py**: Use `is_gpu(device)` instead of `torch.cuda.is_available()`

## Testing

Tested on Intel Arc B580 (12GB VRAM) running on Unraid 7.1.4:
- Docker image builds successfully ✅
- SNAC model loads on `xpu:0` ✅
- TTS generation produces valid 24kHz WAV audio ✅
- `device.py` detects Intel XPU correctly ✅
- llama.cpp SYCL inference: ~20 tok/s ✅

items.title: Fix CI #16: resolve pylint violations and Python 3.13 compatibility
items.body:
CI #16 failed due to pylint violations across 6 files (exit code 1, 9.46/10 score) and Python 3.13 version constraint mismatch. All issues are resolved without disabling pylint globally.

## What's Changed

### Circular import eliminated (`diffsync/models.py` ↔ `diffsync/adapters.py`)
- `_is_zabbix_target()` previously imported `ZabbixRemoteAdapter` at call time to do `isinstance` — causing `R0401` cyclic-import and `C0415` import-outside-toplevel
- Replaced with a marker attribute check: `getattr(adapter, "IS_ZABBIX_REMOTE", False)`
- Added `IS_ZABBIX_REMOTE = True` to `ZabbixRemoteAdapter`; compatible with `MagicMock(spec=ZabbixRemoteAdapter)` in tests

### Deferred imports moved to module top-level
| File | Symbol | Pylint code |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `diffsync/models.py` | `get_zabbix_client_from_config` | C0415 (×3) |
| `diffsync/adapters.py` | `nautobot.dcim.models.Device` | C0415 |
| `jobs.py` | `django.conf.settings`, `DataMapping` | C0415 (×4) |
| `utils/zabbix.py` | `django.conf.settings` | C0415 |

Tests that patched `utils.zabbix.get_zabbix_client_from_config` for model CRUD tests updated to patch `diffsync.models.get_zabbix_client_from_config` — the correct location after top-level import.

### Inline pylint disables for intentional patterns
- `broad-exception-caught` (W0718) on two adapter catch blocks — alongside existing `# noqa: BLE001`
- `too-few-public-methods` (R0903) on `Meta` inner classes in `jobs.py` and test class in `test_zabbix_diff.py`
- `too-many-arguments` / `too-many-locals` (R0913/R0914) on `ZabbixClient.build_interface` and `upsert_host`

### Test cleanup
- All local imports inside test methods moved to module top-level
- Empty-sequence assertion: `list(...) == []` → `not any(...)` (C1803)

### Python 3.13 support (`pyproject.toml` + `poetry.lock`)
- Updated `requires-python` from `>=3.10,<3.13` to `>=3.10,<3.14` to match CI matrix which tests Python 3.13

items.title: feat: CRUD for documents, components, milestones, and templates
items.body:
## Summary

Extends the CLI with four new entity types, each with its own storage quirks.

- `huly documents list/get/create/update/describe` — documents live in teamspaces (not projects); content is a blob ref accessed via Collaborator RPC with field `"content"`
- `huly components list/get/create/update` — project-scoped, optional `--lead` with fuzzy person match
- `huly milestones list/get/create/update` — project-scoped, `--target-date YYYY-MM-DD` parsing, status enum (planned/in-progress/completed)
- `huly templates list/get/describe` — descriptions are inline ProseMirror JSON (not blob refs), so reads/writes go through `TxUpdateDoc` directly; safety check prevents corruption if a blob ref is detected
- Collaborator RPC generalized to `get_content(class_id, object_id, field, blob_ref)` — works for any entity, not just issues

## Context

- Template `describe --set` includes a safety check: if the description field looks like a blob ref (`*-description-*` pattern), it aborts rather than corrupting data by overwriting the ref with raw JSON
- `get_description`/`set_description` remain as convenience wrappers for backward compatibility
- Components and milestones are currently empty in the EFS workspace but the API classes are verified queryable

items.title: Blender 5.1/Python 3.13 compatibility, profile migration, vendoring improvements, and Poppy Playtime profiles
items.body:
### Motivation
- Add compatibility with newer Blender releases (Blender 5.1 / Python 3.13) and make profile storage cross-platform and robust.
- Improve packaging/build reliability by vendoring already-installed distributions when `pip` installation into the distribution target fails.
- Support Blender's deferred property annotations so class auto-registration works with both legacy and newer annotation styles.
- Provide convenient, selectable generic game profiles for Poppy Playtime Chapters 1–5 in the add-on UI.

### Description
- Updated `README.md` and `docs/source/installation.rst` to document Blender 5.1 / Python 3.13 support and cross-platform profile storage behavior.
- Extended `build.py` with `_iter_requirements`, `_requirement_import_name`, and `_copy_installed_distribution` helper functions and changed the requirements installation loop to create `umodel_tools/third_party`, attempt `pip install` per requirement, and fall back to copying an already-installed distribution when pip fails.
- Added support in `umodel_tools/auto_load.py` to detect Blender's deferred property wrapper (`_PropertyDeferred`) and extract `PointerProperty`/`CollectionProperty` dependencies so registration ordering works with deferred annotations.
- Added five generic shim game profiles under `umodel_tools/game_profiles/` for `poppy_playtime_chapter_1` through `poppy_playtime_chapter_5` that reuse the existing `generic` handlers and provide `GAME_NAME`/`GAME_DESCRIPTION` entries.
- Changed `umodel_tools/preferences.py` to store profiles in Blender's per-user config directory via `bpy.utils.user_resource('CONFIG', ...)`, added legacy `Documents/XModel` support and automatic migration via `_migrate_legacy_profiles`, made profile loading search both new and legacy locations with deduplication, and adjusted parsing/attribute assignment accordingly.
- Minor metadata update in `umodel_tools/__init__.py` to add a `doc_url` to `bl_info`.

### Testing
- No automated tests were added or executed as part of this change.

items.title: Rewrite VM: Frame-based architecture, O(1) stack, 3.11-only, 41 differential tests
items.body:
Major architectural refactoring of the Python bytecode interpreter:

Stack discipline: Flip TOS from index 0 to index -1. All stack ops
are now O(1) — append/pop instead of insert(0)/pop(0). Fixes pre-existing
unary op stack leak (insert without pop).

Frame-based execution: Introduce Frame class with explicit frame stack.
CALL pushes a new Frame, RETURN_VALUE pops and resumes caller. Eliminates
host recursion for guest calls — guest call depth is no longer bounded by
Python's recursion limit.

VMFunction: Replace _mf_make() closure factory with VMFunction class that
stores code + defaults + closure metadata without creating a host callable.

Index-based fast locals: Frame.locals_fast is a list indexed by co_varnames
slot. LOAD_FAST/STORE_FAST use direct indexing, no name lookups.

IR via dis.get_instructions: Replace manual bytecode_optimize() with
dis.get_instructions(adaptive=False, show_caches=False). Handles
EXTENDED_ARG, CACHE slots, and jump target resolution automatically.

Correct 3.11 CALL protocol: _NULL sentinel (distinct from None) for
PUSH_NULL. CALL checks PEEK(argc+2) to determine method vs non-method
calls, matching CPython's ceval.c behavior.

Drop Python 2.7: Remove hasarg(), variable-width bytecode path, and all
2.7-only opcodes (PRINT_ITEM, SETUP_LOOP, CALL_FUNCTION variants, etc).

Exception handling: General try/except around opcode dispatch routes
exceptions through the 3.11 exception table.

Remove meta-circular hacks: No more py2vm name patching in STORE_NAME
or CALL. VMFunction/native callable distinction makes this unnecessary.

New opcodes: BUILD_SET, END_FOR, LIST_EXTEND, SET_UPDATE, SET_ADD,
LIST_TO_TUPLE, RAISE_VARARGS, BEFORE_WITH, DELETE_ATTR, DELETE_GLOBAL,
IMPORT_NAME, IMPORT_FROM, IMPORT_STAR. Tier 6 (generators/async) rejected
with NotImplementedError.

Testing: Add test_vm.py differential harness (41 tests) comparing VM
stdout against CPython across Tiers 1-5 — arithmetic, functions, closures,
classes, exceptions, comprehensions, imports, f-strings, etc.

Fix SQLAlchemy compatibility with Python 3.13
items.body:
## Problem

SQLAlchemy 2.0.30 is incompatible with Python 3.13, causing a runtime crash on import with `TypeError: Can't replace canonical symbol for '__firstlineno__' with new int value 615`. This prevents the FastAPI backend from starting at all.

## Solution

Bumped `sqlalchemy` from `2.0.30` to `2.0.36` in `finance-backend/requirements.txt`. Version 2.0.36 includes the fix for Python 3.13's changes to symbol handling in the `__firstlineno__` attribute, restoring compatibility without requiring a major version jump.

### Changes
- **Modified** `finance-backend/requirements.txt`

items.title: fix(compat): respect explicit Field(sa_type=...) in Python 3.14 patch
items.body:
The Python 3.14 compatibility patch (`_patched_get_sqlalchemy_type`) auto-detected `Union[list/dict/tuple/set, None]` annotations as JSONB before checking whether the user had passed an explicit `sa_type` to `Field(...)`. This caused the user override to be silently ignored on Python 3.14+, breaking non-PostgreSQL dialects such as SQLite.

For example:

    options: list[str] | None = Field(default=None, sa_type=JSON)

was resolved to `JSONB` instead of `JSON`, making SQLite DDL generation fail with `Compiler ... can't render element of type JSONB`.

Upstream `sqlmodel.main.get_sqlalchemy_type` checks `field.sa_type` first before any annotation-based detection. This patch restores that precedence ordering by extracting the explicit `sa_type` (from either `FieldInfoMetadata.sa_type` or the direct attribute) at the top of the patched function and returning it immediately when present.

items.title: Fix Python 3.13 compatibility: remove cgi, fix PyG imports, add weights_only to torch.load
items.body:
The codebase used three APIs that are broken on Python 3.13 + modern PyTorch/PyTorch Geometric: the removed `cgi` stdlib module, a relocated PyG loader class, and `torch.load()` without the now-required `weights_only` argument.

## Changes

- **Remove `from cgi import test`** (`exp/exp_GIF.py`, `exp/exp_retrain.py`, `exp/exp_attack.py`, `exp/exp_unlearn_inv.py`)  
  `cgi` was removed entirely in Python 3.13. The symbol was imported but never used in any of these files.

- **Fix `NeighborSampler` import path** (same 4 files)  
  ```python
  # before
  from torch_geometric.data import NeighborSampler
  # after
  from torch_geometric.loader import NeighborSampler
  ```
  PyG moved all loaders to `torch_geometric.loader`; the old path raises `ImportError` in current releases.

- **Add `weights_only=False` to all `torch.load()` calls** (`lib_dataset/data_store.py` ×4, `lib_gnn_model/gnn_base.py` ×1)  
  PyTorch 2.x warns and will eventually break when loading non-tensor checkpoints (dicts, mixed types) without this flag set explicitly.

- **Add `PYTHON313_COMPATIBILITY.md`**  
  Documents every change, rationale, and the compatible version matrix.

items.title: Add Python-version markers to dependency files for Python 3.13+ compatibility
items.body:
### Motivation
- Prevent Python 3.13+ environments from attempting to build legacy `numpy<2.0` from source (which triggers Meson/compiler failures on Windows). 
- Make dependency selection explicit by Python version so non-TensorFlow parts of the project remain installable on newer Python releases. 
- Preserve the project constraint that full Keras/TensorFlow workflows require older Python runtimes (`3.10`/`3.11`) by keeping the TensorFlow marker.

### Description
- Updated `requirements.txt` and `Install_Packages.txt` to add Python-version-aware markers for `numpy`, `scikit-learn`, and `h5py`. 
- Added modern ranges for Python `>=3.13` (`numpy>=2.1,<2.3`, `scikit-learn>=1.6,<1.8`, `h5py>=3.11,<3.14`) while keeping legacy ranges for older Python versions. 
- Clarified compatibility notes in the files and kept the TensorFlow constraint `tensorflow==2.15.1; python_version < "3.12"` to indicate full TF support is limited to older Python versions. 

### Testing
- Ran `python --version` which succeeded and reported the runtime used. 
- Ran `python -m pip --version` which succeeded and reported the pip client used. 
- Ran `python -m pip install --dry-run -r requirements.txt` to verify marker parsing, and the environment parsed conditional dependencies as expected but the attempt to contact the package index failed due to a network/proxy error (dry-run did not complete because of a `403` proxy response). 
- Inspected the resulting files with `nl`/`cat` to verify the expected conditional lines were written into `requirements.txt` and `Install_Packages.txt` successfully.

Bumps [pyasn1](https://github.com/pyasn1/pyasn1) from 0.4.2 to 0.6.3.
Release notes

Sourced from pyasn1's releases.

Release 0.6.3

It's a minor release.

  • Added nesting depth limit to ASN.1 decoder to prevent stack overflow from deeply nested structures (CVE-2026-30922).
  • Fixed OverflowError from oversized BER length field.
  • Fixed DeprecationWarning stacklevel for deprecated attributes.
  • Fixed asDateTime incorrect fractional seconds parsing.

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

Release 0.6.2

It's a minor release.

  • Fixed continuation octet limits in OID/RELATIVE-OID decoder (CVE-2026-23490).
  • Added support for Python 3.14.
  • Added SECURITY.md policy.
  • Migrated to pyproject.toml packaging.

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

Release 0.6.1

It's a minor release.

  • Added support for Python 3.13.
  • Cleaned Python 2-related code.
  • Removed bdist_wheel universal flag from setup.cfg.

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

Release 0.6.0

It's a major release where we drop Python 2 support entirely. The most significant changes are:

  • Removed support for EOL Python 2.7, 3.6, 3.7
  • Added support for previously missing RELATIVE-OID construct
  • Updated link to Layman's Guide

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

Release 0.5.1

It's a minor release.

  • Added support for PyPy 3.10 and Python 3.12
  • Updated RTD configuration to include a dummy index.rst redirecting to contents.html, ensuring compatibility with third-party documentation and search indexes.
  • Fixed the API breakage wih decoder.decode(substrateFun=...). A substrateFun passed to decoder.decode() can now be either v0.4 Non-Streaming or v0.5 Streaming. pyasn1 will detect and handle both cases transparently. A substrateFun passed to one of the new streaming decoders is still expected to be v0.5 Streaming only.

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

## Summary
- Add `from __future__ import annotations` for Python 3.9 compatibility (`str | None` syntax requires 3.10+)
- Validate socket file existence before using `KITTY_LISTEN_ON` (env var may point to stale socket after kitty restart)

Background processes launched via `--type=background` may inherit a stale `KITTY_LISTEN_ON` pointing to an old PID's socket. items.title: Refactor phytron_phymotion for standalone operation and Python3 compatibility
items.body:
### Motivation
- Remove legacy external dependencies (`slave`, `e21_util`) and provide a minimal, self-contained serial driver stack. 
- Modernize code for Python 3 compatibility (bytes/str handling, removal of `long`/`basestring`, use of `__bool__`).
- Provide a clear architecture overview and a small transport adapter to hide `pyserial` details. 

### Description
- Add `ARCHITECTURE.md` documenting package structure and dataflow. 
- Introduce `SerialTransport` in `phytron_phymotion/transport.py` as a thin `pyserial` adapter exposing `write`, `read_until`, and `read_bytes`. 
- Add `errors.py` with a `CommunicationError` exception and remove dependence on `e21_util.error`. 
- Replace earlier protocol implementation with `phytron_phymotion/protocol.py` that uses a `threading.RLock`, in-process locking, simplified `query`/`read_response` flow, and returns `None` for invalid message types instead of raising. 
- Update `message.py` to be Python 3 safe by replacing `long`/`basestring` checks with `int`/`str`, implement `__bool__`, and tidy response handling. 
- Modify `driver.py` to remove external framework base classes, adapt to the new protocol/transport interfaces, fix small bugs in sign handling and `move_absolute`, and return `None` for missing responses. 
- Update `factory.py` to use `SerialTransport`, standard `logging`, and use `os.environ['PHYTRON_DEVICE']` fallback for device selection. 
- Update `setup.py` to declare `pyserial` as the installation requirement and remove legacy requirements. 

### Testing
- No automated tests were executed as part of this change.

- Converted multiple modules to use Python 3 idioms: relative imports (e.g. `from .windows`), `print()` calls, `next()` iterator usage, `io.BytesIO`, `urllib.parse.unquote`, and `str`/`bytes` handling (decoding `stdout`/`stderr` when needed). 
- Replaced Python 2 type checks and builtins with modern equivalents (`unicode/long` -> `str/int`, `type(...) in [...]` -> `isinstance(...)`, and added safe `unicode` fallback in `utils.py`). 
- Made optional binary/unrar integrations robust: conditional imports for `unrar` and `PyPDF2`, returning sensible fallbacks when not present, and updated `OpenableRarFile` to return bytes. 
- Improved UnRAR2 unix/windows implementations for Python 3 (bytes decoding, `DeprecationWarning` invocation, next() calls) and updated the test harness to use `pytest` with a skip when `unrar/rar` is not installed. 
- Added a minimal web UI under `webui/` with a small HTTP API and ComicVine client: endpoints for scanning (`/api/scan`), reading (`/api/read`), writing (`/api/write`) metadata and searching ComicVine (`/api/comicvine/search`), plus `webui/server.py` entrypoint. 
- Miscellaneous safe-guards and bugfixes: ensure ComicArchive logo default handling, natsort fallback, and numerous stderr/stdout error message fixes to be Python 3 compatible. 

### Testing
- Added a tiny pytest test `tests/test_sanity.py::test_sanity` as a basic runnable test; running `pytest -q` executes this test and it passes. 
- The UnRAR2 integration test file was adapted to `pytest` and will `pytest.skip` when `unrar`/`rar` is not available, so CI environments without an unpacker will not fail. 
- No other automated tests were broken by these changes in local runs (integration UnRAR2 tests run when the `unrar/rar` executable is available and will validate password/error handling as before).

items.title: Fix 20+ bugs across PowerShell GUI, CLI, and Python backend
items.body:
PowerShell GUI (KUMO-Label-Manager.ps1):
- Fix WinForms dock order causing scrambled panel layout
- Fix Find & Replace counting all labels instead of matched ones
- Fix Auto-Number applying in reverse order on selected rows
- Fix null reference crash in search filter
- Fix regex backreference bug in replace (use literal String.Replace)
- Remove dead code (Invoke-SecureRestMethod, Get-KumoRouterName)

PowerShell CLI (KUMO-Excel-Updater.ps1):
- Fix Telnet fallback being dead code (REST swallowed all errors)
- Fix Telnet hardcoded 32 ports (now uses detected port count)
- Fix resource leaks with finally blocks for TCP cleanup
- Remove dead code (Invoke-SecureRestMethod)

Python backend:
- Fix cli.py using non-existent settings.kumo_host (now router_ip)
- Fix cli.py using non-existent label.label_text (now current_label)
- Fix EventBus using sync 'with' on asyncio.Lock (now async with)
- Fix BaseAgent calling async subscribe methods without await
- Fix api_agent missing await on _emit_labels_event calls
- Fix rest_client only checking inputs for real labels (now checks both)
- Fix rest_client shadowing builtin ConnectionError/TimeoutError
- Fix schema rejecting valid INPUT+OUTPUT with same port number
- Fix Python 3.8 compat (str|Path -> Union, list[] -> List[])
- Fix settings port range 1-32 -> 1-64 for 64-port routers

## Summary by Sourcery

Fix multiple issues across the PowerShell tools and Python backend to improve label handling reliability, router compatibility, and event-driven coordination.

Bug Fixes:
- Allow Telnet label queries and default templates in the PowerShell CLI to respect the detected router port count instead of a hardcoded 32 ports.
- Ensure PowerShell CLI Telnet connections and streams are always cleaned up using finally blocks, and surface REST update failures so callers can fall back to Telnet.
- Correct WinForms dock ordering in the PowerShell GUI so panels render in the intended layout.
- Prevent null reference errors and incorrect matches in the PowerShell GUI search and find/replace logic, and ensure auto-numbering applies in a predictable row order.
- Update the Python CLI to use the correct router_ip setting and current_label field when reading labels.
- Fix the event bus to use async locks consistently and make subscription management methods asynchronous, with BaseAgent awaiting subscribe/unsubscribe calls.
- Ensure the API agent awaits label event emission and the REST client treats either input or output non-default labels as a successful REST download.
- Adjust schema validation to allow matching INPUT and OUTPUT ports with the same number and raise on true duplicate port/type pairs.
- Expand supported port counts and label file schema limits to handle up to 64-port routers (128 total ports in files).
- Restore Python 3.8 compatibility by replacing PEP 604 and builtin generic type annotations with typing.Union and typing.List.
- Avoid shadowing builtin ConnectionError and TimeoutError by renaming REST client exceptions.

Enhancements:
- Remove unused helper functions from the PowerShell GUI and CLI scripts.
- Clarify and centralize form control additions in the PowerShell GUI to make dock behavior explicit and maintainable.

items.title: Replace selenium-wire with Chrome DevTools network logging

### Motivation
- The original implementation used `selenium-wire`, which pulls in transitive packages that cause `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pkg_resources'` under Python 3.12, so the network-capture approach was changed to avoid that dependency.
- Using Selenium 4's Chrome DevTools logs provides a lightweight, dependency-free way to inspect request headers from the browser session.

### Description
- Replaced `selenium-wire` request capture with Selenium 4 Chrome DevTools performance logs and CDP `Network.enable` in `src/extract_eqs_tokens.py` and removed the `seleniumwire` import and proxy usage.

- Switched `build_driver` to enable Chrome performance logging and use `driver.execute_cdp_cmd("Network.enable", {})` to capture network events. 
- Removed `selenium-wire`, `blinker`, and `setuptools` entries from `requirements.txt` and updated `README.md` to document the new CDP-based approach and the Python 3.12 compatibility benefit; the files changed are `src/extract_eqs_tokens.py`, `requirements.txt`, and `README.md`.

items.title: Fix sync errors and index corruption in instabackup
items.body:
I have addressed the "File not found" and sync errors by refactoring the synchronization logic and the backup index structure. 

The main issue was that the script was deleting bookmarks as soon as it saw a deletion event from the Instapaper API, without considering that the bookmark might have been moved to another folder. This caused "File not found" errors when the script later tried to move the already-deleted file, or led to unnecessary re-downloads.

I also fixed an issue where trailing spaces in filenames (from article titles) could cause "File not found" errors when appending extensions.

Key changes:
1. **New Index Structure**: Separated bookmark paths and folder contents to prevent ID collisions. Added a migration path for old indexes.
2. **Multi-pass Sync**: The script now fetches the state of all folders first. This allows it to identify bookmarks that were moved rather than deleted, ensuring they are correctly moved on disk and not prematurely removed.
3. **Improved Filename Sanitization**: Added `.strip()` to the slugify function to handle titles with trailing spaces.
4. **Resilient Deletion**: The script now only deletes files for bookmarks that are no longer present in *any* folder after the full sync is complete. It also handles cases where the file is already missing.
5. **Python 3.12 Compatibility**: Added `setuptools` to dependencies to provide the `distutils` layer required by some of the package's dependencies.

I've verified these changes with a new test suite that covers both index migration and complex bookmark movement scenarios.

items.title: Update pandas and streamlit for Python 3.13 compatibility
items.body:
Streamlit Cloud deployment fails on Python 3.13.9 because pandas 2.0.3 and numpy 1.24.3 lack pre-built wheels, causing source builds that fail (numpy requires removed `distutils`).

## Changes

- **pandas**: `==2.0.3` → `>=2.1.0` (first version with Python 3.13 wheels)
- **streamlit**: `==1.31.0` → `>=1.31.0` (allows pip to select compatible versions)
- **numpy**: already `>=1.26.0` from previous update (first version with Python 3.13 support)

**Before:**
```
pandas==2.0.3      # Build fails: no Python 3.13 wheels
streamlit==1.31.0  # Pinned too tightly
numpy>=1.26.0      # ✓ Already correct
```

**After:**
```
pandas>=2.1.0      # ✓ Python 3.13 wheels available
streamlit>=1.31.0  # ✓ Flexible versioning
numpy>=1.26.0      # ✓ No change needed
```

All updated packages have pre-built wheels for Python 3.13, eliminating build-time compilation.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> ## Problem
> 
> The Streamlit app is failing to deploy with build errors for both pandas and numpy:
> 
> ```
> Building wheel for pandas (pyproject.toml): finished with status 'error'
> Building wheel for numpy (pyproject.toml): finished with status 'error'
> ```
> 
> The deployment uses Python 3.13.9, but:
> - `pandas==2.0.3` doesn't have pre-built wheels for Python 3.13
> - `numpy==1.24.3` requires `distutils` (removed in Python 3.12) and doesn't have pre-built wheels for Python 3.13
> 
> ## Solution
> 
> Update `requirements.txt` to use newer versions that have pre-built wheels compatible with Python 3.13:
> 
> **Current versions:**
> ```
> streamlit==1.31.0
> pandas==2.0.3
> python-dotenv==1.0.0
> google-generativeai>=0.3.0
> numpy==1.24.3
> ```
> 
> **Updated versions:**
> ```
> streamlit>=1.31.0
> pandas>=2.1.0
> python-dotenv==1.0.0
> google-generativeai>=0.3.0
> numpy>=1.26.0
> ```
> 
> Key changes:
> - **numpy**: Update to `>=1.26.0` (first version with Python 3.13 support)
> - **pandas**: Update to `>=2.1.0` (has pre-built wheels for Python 3.13)
> - **streamlit**: Change to `>=1.31.0` to allow compatible versions
> 
> These versions all have pre-built wheels for Python 3.13, which will prevent build failures and speed up deployment.
> 
> ## Additional Context
> 
> - Environment: Python 3.13.9
> - Both packages are trying to build from source instead of using pre-built wheels
> - Pre-built wheels are essential for successful Streamlit Cloud deployment

items.title: Unblock backend startup dependencies and replace broken frontend link with a runnable API dashboard
items.body:
The issue required getting backend and frontend running, fixing runtime errors, restoring frontend/backend connectivity, and ensuring UI actions produce visible outputs. This PR addresses startup blockers and introduces a minimal, consistent frontend surface wired to live backend endpoints.

- **Backend dependency unblock (Python 3.12/runtime compatibility)**
  - Updated dependency pins in `backend/requirements.txt` to resolve install/runtime conflicts that prevented backend startup:
    - `numpy==1.24.3` → `numpy==1.26.4`
    - `slowapi==0.1.4` → `slowapi==0.1.9` (compatible with `redis==5.0.1` in this repo)

- **Frontend restoration (broken gitlink → runnable app)**
  - Replaced the `frontend` gitlink entry with an actual tracked frontend project.
  - Added a minimal Vite app:
    - `frontend/package.json`
    - `frontend/package-lock.json`
    - `frontend/index.html`
    - `frontend/main.js`
    - `frontend/styles.css`

- **UI consistency + endpoint wiring**
  - Added a consistent single-page dashboard layout with:
    - Menu tabs: **Health / Metrics / Streaming**
    - Action buttons mapped to backend endpoints
    - Output panel for API responses/errors
  - Ensured non-duplicative button behavior (distinct endpoint mapping for system action button).

- **Backend/Frontend API connectivity**
  - Frontend now calls backend endpoints using configurable base URL (`http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/v1` by default).
  - Wired actions to:
    - `/health`
    - `/metrics`
    - `/metrics/precision-recall`
    - `/metrics/live`

items.title: Add comprehensive unit tests for util.py + Python 3 compatibility
items.body:
# Add comprehensive unit tests for util.py + Python 3 compatibility

## Summary
This PR introduces 31 unit tests for the `util.py` module and makes it Python 3 compatible by converting print statements to print() function calls. The tests cover:
- Distance calculations (kilDist, mileDist, meterDist, findArc, euclidean)
- Coordinate conversions (getMeters, getCoord)  
- Helper functions (getLineForItems, getTimeDeltas, addIfKey, getIfKey)
- Edge cases (antipodal points, date line crossing, floating point precision)

External dependencies (geopy, xlrd) are stubbed in tests to avoid network calls and missing packages. All 31 tests pass with Python 3.12.

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human
⚠️ **CRITICAL: Partial Python 2 → 3 Migration Risk**

- [ ] **Verify Python 2 compatibility**: The print() changes may break Python 2 compatibility. If Python 2 support is required, add `from __future__ import print_function` at the top of util.py before these changes can be safely merged.
- [ ] **Run the full application**: Verify that other modules (especially processStops.py, classes.py) can still import and use util.py without errors. The codebase is Python 2, but util.py is now Python 3 syntax.
- [ ] **Run the test suite**: Execute `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p "test_*.py"` to confirm all 31 tests pass in your environment.
- [ ] **Consider migration strategy**: This PR makes only util.py Python 3 compatible while the rest of the codebase remains Python 2. Decide if you want to continue with full Python 3 migration or maintain Python 2.7 compatibility with appropriate imports.

### Test Plan
```bash
# Run unit tests
cd /path/to/bhulan
python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p "test_*.py" -v

# Verify all 31 tests pass
# Expected output: Ran 31 tests in ~0.005s, OK
```

### Notes
- Tests stub geopy and xlrd dependencies to avoid external dependencies and network calls
- Only pure functions in util.py are tested; database-dependent modules (classes.py, processStops.py) remain untested
- The codebase has other Python 2-isms (e.g., classes.py has a `self.self.item[CONF_KEY]` bug) that are not addressed in this PR
- This is a foundation for future test coverage; integration tests with MongoDB can be added as a follow-up

items.title: Fix backend setup for local dev: remove slugify dependency and document Python version limits
items.body:
### Motivation

- Remove the runtime `ModuleNotFoundError: slugify` startup failure by eliminating the external `python-slugify` dependency. 
- Prevent build issues with `pydantic-core` when users run Python `3.14` by documenting the known PyO3 compatibility limit. 
- Improve local developer experience and reproducibility for the backend setup.

### Description

- Replaced external `python-slugify` usage with an internal slug generator in `backend/app/services/slug.py` that uses `unicodedata` + regex and preserves unique-suffix logic (`-2`, `-3`, ...). 
- Removed `python-slugify` from `backend/requirements.txt` to avoid the missing-module runtime error. 
- Added a clear Python version compatibility note to `backend/README.md` recommending `3.11–3.13` and warning about `3.14`/`pydantic-core`/PyO3 issues. 
- Propagated the same Python version guidance to the repo `README.md` so local setup instructions surface the warning early.

### Testing

- Ran `python -m compileall backend/app` which completed successfully and validated the Python modules. 
- Removed `__pycache__` artifacts as part of the validation and committed the changes.

items.title: fix: Use APP_VERSION env var for health version reporting, add cspell
items.body:
## Fix version reporting in health endpoint

### Changes

- **health.py**: Replace `_get_version()` file reader with `request.app.version` (sourced from `APP_VERSION` env var set by Docker build)
- **health.py**: Fix Python 3.10 compatibility (`datetime.UTC` → `datetime.timezone.utc`)
- **test_health.py**: Update assertion to match `Config` default version
- **PROJECT_PLAN.md**: Mark Phase 2 completed, Phase 3 as next
- **cspell.json**: Add spell checking configuration
- **.pre-commit-config.yaml**: Add cSpell hook

### Why

The previous `_get_version()` read from a `VERSION` file not present in the Docker container, always returning `"unknown"`. The `APP_VERSION` env var is correctly set during Docker build (`VERSION_FILE.run_number`).

### Verification

After merge and Docker build, update k8s-gitops to the new version:

```bash
curl -s https://api.lab.informationcart.com/health | jq '.version'
# Expected: "1.0.6"
```

items.title: Fix seed parameter TypeError on Python < 3.10
items.body:
The `simulate()` function used the `int | None` union type syntax (PEP 604) introduced in Python 3.10, causing a `TypeError` at import time on Python 3.9 and below.

## Changes

- **`game_of_life.py`**: Replace `seed: int | None = None` with `seed: Optional[int] = None` using `typing.Optional` for compatibility with Python 3.7+
- **`README.md`**: Fix incorrect "GPU implementation" description; add accurate project overview and CLI usage docs for both `game_of_life.py` and `render_animation.py`

```python
# Before (Python 3.10+ only)
def simulate(..., seed: int | None = None) -> None:

# After (Python 3.7+)
from typing import Optional
def simulate(..., seed: Optional[int] = None) -> None:
```
